title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Automation and Data Client Sys… · Behavior Case: Problem Details
summary: A compliance collector encounters accepted jobs, no-content results, partial ranges, problem details, and uncertain remote effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py problem details increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse RFC 9457 problem details by type and extensions without scraping human-readable title or detail text. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
